back_translation: Therefore, O man, you who judge another, every one who judges has no excuse — because in the matter in which you judge another, you are condemning yourself, because you who judge are doing the same thing
And we know that the (royal) judgment of God against those who do such things is according to the truth
Do you think this, O man, you who judge those doing such things while doing the same thing yourself — that you will escape the (royal) judgment of God?
Or do you despise the riches of His (royal) kindness, His patience, and His (royal) restraint of mind — not knowing that the (royal) kindness of God leads you toward repentance?

But according to your hardness and your heart-mind that does not repent, you are storing up (royal) wrath for yourself — for the day of (royal) wrath and the revealing of the righteous (royal) judgment of God
He, 'who (royally) repays everyone according to his deeds'
To those who, by perseverance in doing good, seek (royal) glory, honor, and imperishability — He will (royally) grant eternal life
But to those who act out of self-interest, reject the truth, and obey unrighteousness — (royal) wrath and (royal) anger will come
Affliction and distress will come to every life of a human who does evil — to the Jew first, and to the Greek as well
But (royal) glory, honor, and peace will come to everyone who does good — to the Jew first, and to the Greek as well
For God does not (royally) choose-faces (=show favoritism) toward anyone
For all who sin without the Law will also perish without the Law; and all who sin under the Law will be judged by the Law
For it is not the hearers of the Law who are righteous in the sight of God, but those who conduct themselves according to the Law are the ones who will be (royally) reckoned as righteous
For when Gentiles who do not have the Law do what the Law demands by nature, those without the Law are themselves a Law to themselves
They are those who show that the work of the Law has been written in their heart-minds, with their conscience bearing witness together with them, and their thoughts accusing and defending one another
On the day when God will (royally) judge the secrets of humans according to my good news, through Jesus Christ
But if you call yourself a Jew, and rely upon the Law, and boast in God
And know His (royal) will, and approve of what is more excellent — because you have been instructed from the Law
And are confident that you yourself are a guide for the blind, a light for those who are in darkness
An instructor of the senseless, a teacher of innocent children, possessing in the Law the form of knowledge and truth
Therefore, you who teach others — do you not teach yourself? You who proclaim 'do not steal' — do you steal?
You who forbid committing adultery — do you commit adultery? You who detest idols — do you rob temples?
You who boast in the Law — do you dishonor God by transgressing the Law?
Just as it is written: 'The (royal) name of God is being blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you'
Circumcision is beneficial if you conduct yourself according to the Law; but if you are a transgressor of the Law, your circumcision has become non-circumcision
Therefore, if the one who is not circumcised keeps the regulations of the Law, will not his non-circumcision be (royally) reckoned as circumcision?
And the one who is naturally not-circumcised but does the Law will judge you, the one who, although having the letter of the Law and circumcision, is yet a transgressor of the Law
For it is not the one who is a Jew outwardly who is a Jew, and it is not circumcision that is outward in the flesh that is true circumcision

But it is the one who is a Jew inwardly who is the true Jew, and circumcision of the heart-mind — by the (royal) Spirit, not by the letter — is the true circumcision; the praise of such a person does not come from humans, but comes from God
